definition: New Week Opening Gap (NWOG), New Day Opening Gap (NDOG), and New Month Opening Gap (NMOG) — price voids between session closes and opens that act as liquidity magnets and institutional reference levels. The 50% midpoint (Consequent Encroachment) is the highest-probability reaction level.
depends_on: liquidity_void (opening gaps ARE liquidity voids — specific time-based instances)
feeds_into: consequent_encroachment (CE of opening gaps is the precision entry level)

nwog.definition: Gap between Friday 16:00 ET close and Sunday 18:00 ET open

behavior.first_half_of_week: Price is drawn to fill the NWOG — expect Monday/Tuesday to trade into the gap
behavior.ce_reaction: NWOG CE (50%) is the most reliable reaction level of the entire week
behavior.full_fill: ~70% of NWOGs fill completely within the first 3 days
behavior.direction_clue: If price opens above NWOG and fills down to CE → bullish bias for rest of week (and vice versa)

nwog.significance: HIGHEST priority opening gap — sets the tone for the entire week
nwog.crypto_note: Crypto has no true weekend close, but CME BTC/ETH futures DO close Friday 17:00 ET and open Sunday 18:00 ET — use CME gap

ndog.definition: Gap between previous day 17:00 ET close and current day 18:00 ET open (forex). For equities: previous close to current open.

behavior.morning_magnet: Price is drawn to NDOG CE during London/NY AM sessions
behavior.fill_rate: ~80% of NDOGs fill within the same trading day
behavior.smaller_gaps: NDOGs are smaller than NWOGs — less significance but higher fill probability

ndog.significance: Daily reference level — useful for intraday bias confirmation

nmog.definition: Gap between previous month's last trading day close and new month's first trading day open

behavior.monthly_magnet: NMOG CE acts as a macro reference for the entire month
behavior.quarterly_alignment: NMOGs at quarter boundaries (Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct) are especially significant
behavior.fill_timeline: May take 1-3 weeks to fill — use for swing trade bias

nmog.significance: Macro-level reference — less actionable for intraday but critical for swing bias

trading_rules.rule_1: Always calculate the CE (50%) of every opening gap — this is your primary reaction level
trading_rules.rule_2: NWOG CE is the strongest level of the week — plan trades around it
trading_rules.rule_3: If price is above NWOG, bias is bullish (buying dips to CE). Below = bearish.
trading_rules.rule_4: Opening gaps that align with HTF FVGs or order blocks = highest confluence
trading_rules.rule_5: Don't trade against an unfilled opening gap — it acts as a magnet until filled
trading_rules.rule_6: Multiple unfilled gaps stacking = strong directional pressure toward gap zone

event_horizon.definition: The 50% distance between two consecutive NWOGs — acts as macro premium/discount reference
event_horizon.usage: Price above Event Horizon = premium (look for shorts). Below = discount (look for longs).

bridge_integration.auto_calculation: Compute NWOG from Friday close / Sunday open prices; NDOG from daily session boundaries
bridge_integration.level_injection: Add NWOG CE, NDOG CE as session_levels for DOL targeting
bridge_integration.score_impact: Price at NWOG/NDOG CE = +5 to ICT score (high-probability reaction zone)
bridge_integration.bias_filter: Price position relative to NWOG CE informs daily_bias direction
